title: Pellionia lanceolata
taxonomy: Pellionia lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It is a member of the genus Pellionia, which contains approximately 45 species. This genus is part of the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant is native to Southeast Asia, where it is often found in shaded, humid environments. Pellionia lanceolata is a relatively rare species and is not widely cultivated or studied.
uses: Pellionia lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. Pellionia lanceolata has been found to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal properties.

intro: Pellionia lanceolata, also known as the "heartleaf" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. Traditionally, P. lanceolata has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of P. lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, P. lanceolata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or made into a tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
